tldr: Mega-cap technology and AI continue to dominate congressional portfolios, with massive cluster buying in AAPL, NVDA, and MSFT representing tens of millions in notional volume. However, notable individual moves stand out against the broad tech accumulation: Rep. Chip Roy executed a massive $5M+ buy in energy firm AESI, and Rep. Nancy Pelosi initiated large new positions in INTC and UBER. Meanwhile, some members are taking profits in semiconductors and medical devices, highlighted by Rep. Sara Jacobs selling QCOM, Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse trimming NVDA, and Sen. Tina Smith exiting PODD and DXCM.

highlights.headline: Massive $5M+ Buy in AESI
highlights.detail: Rep. Chip Roy disclosed an unusually large purchase of Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) valued between $5.0M and $25.0M on April 30, dwarfing typical congressional trade sizes and signaling a massive conviction bet in the energy sector.

highlights.headline: Pelosi Initiates Large INTC and UBER Positions
highlights.detail: Rep. Nancy Pelosi disclosed a $1.0M–$5.0M purchase of Intel (INTC) and a $500K–$1.0M purchase of Uber (UBER) in late May, signaling fresh capital deployment into a legacy semiconductor turnaround and ride-share mobility.

highlights.headline: Relentless Accumulation of Mega-Cap Tech
highlights.detail: Congress continues to pile into AI and mega-cap tech, led by Apple ($32.7M notional across 27 members), Nvidia ($10.6M across 17 members), and Microsoft ($5.9M across 30 members).

highlights.headline: Trimming Winners in QCOM and NVDA
highlights.detail: While the broader cohort buys tech, Rep. Sara Jacobs sold between $1.0M and $2.0M of Qualcomm (QCOM) across two trades, and Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse trimmed up to $500K of Nvidia (NVDA), indicating targeted profit-taking in semiconductors.

highlights.headline: Coordinated Sells in Medical Devices
highlights.detail: Sen. Tina Smith's spouse executed multiple $100K–$250K sales of medical device makers Insulet (PODD) and DexCom (DXCM) throughout late April and early May, completely stepping away from these specific diabetes-care names.

highlights.headline: High-Volume Trading in Microsoft
highlights.detail: Rep. Josh Gottheimer executed multiple simultaneous buys and sells of Microsoft (MSFT) in the $250K–$1.0M brackets on a single day, indicating active portfolio rebalancing or options-related assignments rather than a directional bet.
